`After=` the daemon unit **and** actively polling `/sys/class/net/wanbond0` in
its own `ExecStartPre` until the interface exists, so it never races tun
creation regardless of how long the daemon takes to create it.

Once `tun_persist = true` (above) is enabled, `wanbond0` and the
addresses/routes/rules attached to it survive a daemon restart on their own,
so re-running this oneshot on every restart becomes **optional but
harmless** — keep it enabled anyway as a safety net for what `tun_persist`
does not cover (first boot, a manual `ip link delete wanbond0`, a host that
cannot enable `tun_persist`).

## 5. Firewall

### Concentrator: UDP listen port

Allow the WireGuard UDP port in from the WAN (and, on OCI, in the subnet's
security list / NSG as well — both layers must pass):

```sh
iptables -I INPUT -p udp --dport 51820 -j ACCEPT
```

### Concentrator: tunnel-interface ACCEPT — REQUIRED (OCI default-REJECT caveat)

The concentrator **must ACCEPT traffic arriving on the wanbond tunnel
interface, ordered ahead of any default REJECT rule**:

```sh
iptables -I INPUT -i wanbond0 -j ACCEPT
```

Why this is required: Oracle Cloud (OCI) images ship an INPUT chain ending in

```
-A INPUT -j REJECT --reject-with icmp-host-prohibited
```

which silently applies to the tunnel interface too. ICMP echo is allowed by an
earlier rule, so **ping through the tunnel works while any TCP connection
through the tunnel fails with a confusing `No route to host`** (the REJECT's
ICMP host-prohibited answer). This exact failure was hit during P0 real-host
testing. `iptables -I` (insert at head) places the ACCEPT ahead of the appended
(`-A`) REJECT; verify the ordering with `iptables -S INPUT` — the tunnel ACCEPT
must appear before the `-j REJECT` line. The same applies to any distro or
provider whose default firewall ends INPUT with REJECT/DROP (e.g. firewalld
zones): treat "tunnel ACCEPT ahead of the default reject" as a required
concentrator deployment step.

### Concentrator: persist the rules across reboots — REQUIRED

`iptables -I` mutates only the **runtime** chain. On OCI Ubuntu the
`netfilter-persistent` boot service **restores `/etc/iptables/rules.v4`**, so a
reboot silently discards any runtime-only rule and the tunnel ACCEPT (and the
UDP-port ACCEPT) revert to the default REJECT with no signal until
re-provisioned (defect D7). The rules **must** therefore be written to the boot
rules file. On Debian/Ubuntu:

```sh
sudo apt-get install -y iptables-persistent   # provides netfilter-persistent
sudo netfilter-persistent save                # snapshots the runtime chain to /etc/iptables/rules.v4
```

(`service iptables save`, or a firewalld permanent rule, on EL.) Verify the
tunnel ACCEPT survives by confirming it appears in the saved file:

```sh
sudo grep -- '-A INPUT -i wanbond0 -j ACCEPT' /etc/iptables/rules.v4
```

1	# wanbond.example.toml — exhaustive annotated configuration reference.
2	#
3	# Every key wanbond reads is present here. Copy this file, fill the <placeholders>,
4	# and delete what you do not need:
5	#
6	#   sudo install -m 0600 /dev/null /etc/wanbond/edge.toml
7	#   $EDITOR /etc/wanbond/edge.toml     # paste + edit this content
8	#
9	# 0600 is MANDATORY: the file holds the WireGuard private key and the PSK, so
10	# config.Load refuses any file whose mode is not exactly 0600.
11	#
12	# This example is EDGE-oriented. For a CONCENTRATOR: set role = "concentrator",
13	# uncomment [wireguard].listen_port, and REMOVE the peer `endpoint`/`endpoints`
14	# (the concentrator learns the edge's endpoints from authenticated traffic).
15	#
16	# Required keys are shown live; optional keys are commented-out with their
17	# DEFAULT value (uncommenting one at its shown value is a no-op). Cross-field
18	# constraints a single example cannot express inline are listed at the bottom.
19	# Full prose reference: docs/install.md §3z. Everything here is enforced at
20	# config load (fail-fast on the first violation) except the loopback + allowed_ips
21	# checks noted below.
22	
23	# ── top level ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24	role = "edge"                      # REQUIRED. "edge" | "concentrator". Never
25	                                   #   inferred from other keys.
26	psk  = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # REQUIRED. 32 raw bytes, base64. SAME value on
27	                                   #   both ends; keys the PSK-HMAC that
28	                                   #   authenticates outer PROBE/CONTROL frames.
29	                                   #   Generate: head -c 32 /dev/urandom | base64
30	# tun_persist = false              # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT false. When false the TUN
31	                                   #   wanbond0 is destroyed on daemon stop, so its
32	                                   #   addresses/routes/rules are dropped on every
33	                                   #   restart (today's teardown semantics). Set
34	                                   #   true and the daemon issues TUNSETPERSIST on
35	                                   #   start: wanbond0 SURVIVES a daemon restart
36	                                   #   with the SAME ifindex, so operator-owned
37	                                   #   addressing persists untouched. NOTE (D39): a
38	                                   #   persistent device STILL needs the
39	                                   #   NetworkManager unmanaged-devices drop-in on
40	                                   #   NM hosts — persistence keeps the link, it
41	                                   #   does not exempt it from NM.
42	# bind = "auto"                    # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT "auto". Top-level default
43	                                   #   bind mode, applied to every [[paths]] block
44	                                   #   that omits its own `bind` below. "source" |
45	                                   #   "device" | "auto" — see the per-path `bind`
46	                                   #   note below for what each value does. Exists
47	                                   #   for the VLAN-per-WAN policy-routing case;
48	                                   #   see docs/install.md §3b.
49	
50	# ── paths: one [[paths]] block per WAN uplink; at least one is REQUIRED ───────
51	[[paths]]
52	name        = "starlink"           # REQUIRED. Stable, unique identifier.
53	source_addr = "192.168.1.10"       # REQUIRED. Bare local source IP the path's UDP
54	                                   #   socket binds to. Must be unique across paths
55	                                   #   (a shared source collides EADDRINUSE at the
56	                                   #   second bind). No default.
57	# bind = "auto"                    # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT the top-level default above
58	                                   #   (itself DEFAULT "auto" when that is also
59	                                   #   omitted). "source" forces the pre-T16
60	                                   #   source-IP pin unconditionally (defeats
61	                                   #   device-bind roam tolerance, but is the fix
62	                                   #   for a one-address-per-VLAN policy-routing
63	                                   #   edge — see docs/install.md §3b). "device"
64	                                   #   forces SO_BINDTODEVICE unconditionally.
65	                                   #   "auto" reproduces today's heuristic:
66	                                   #   device-bind only when provably equivalent
67	                                   #   to the source-IP pin, source-bind otherwise.
68	# dest_addr = "203.0.113.7:51820"  # OPTIONAL, edge-only meaning. Per-path
69	                                   #   concentrator endpoint (ip:port). Omit when
70	                                   #   one public IP fronts all uplinks (the peer's
71	                                   #   endpoint is reused). No default. Inert on the
72	                                   #   concentrator (it learns edge endpoints).
73	# link_bandwidth = "50Mbit"        # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared bottleneck
74	                                   #   bandwidth. SI bit/s: k/M/G = 1e3/1e6/1e9
75	                                   #   ("bit" may be written "bps"). Must be > 0.
76	                                   #   Used ONLY under weighted policy + pacing;
77	                                   #   inert otherwise. No default (undeclared).
78	# link_rtt = "45ms"                # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared baseline RTT (Go
79	                                   #   duration). REQUIRED (> 0) when link_bandwidth
80	                                   #   is set under weighted pacing; else ignored.
81	
82	# A second uplink (edge). Repeat the block per path.
83	[[paths]]
84	name        = "5g"
85	source_addr = "192.168.2.10"
86	
87	# ── wireguard: inner tunnel key material ─────────────────────────────────────
88	[wireguard]
89	private_key = "<base64 32-byte private key>"  # REQUIRED (both roles).
90	                                   #   Generate: wg genkey (public: … | wg pubkey)
91	# listen_port = 51820              # CONCENTRATOR-ONLY: REQUIRED (> 0) there; omit /
92	                                   #   leave 0 on the edge. uint16 (< 1024 needs
93	                                   #   CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE).
94	
95	# ── wireguard peers: at least one [[wireguard.peers]] is REQUIRED ─────────────
96	[[wireguard.peers]]
97	public_key = "<base64 peer public key>"  # REQUIRED. base64 32-byte key (the OTHER
98	                                   #   end's public key).
99	endpoint = "203.0.113.7:51820"     # EDGE: REQUIRED (this OR `endpoints`). ip:port
100	                                   #   literal (default) OR hostname with opt-in
101	                                   #   `dns = true` below. CONCENTRATOR: must be
102	                                   #   ABSENT (rejected) — it roams the edge
103	                                   #   dynamically. Mutually exclusive with
104	                                   #   `endpoints`.
105	# dns = false                       # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT false. Set true to enable
106	                                   #   hostname resolution for the endpoint above.
107	                                   #   The [dns] block below is OPTIONAL: it only
108	                                   #   selects the resolver TRANSPORT and defaults
109	                                   #   to the system resolver when absent.
110	# endpoints = ["203.0.113.7:51820", "198.51.100.7:51820"]
111	                                   # EDGE-ONLY ordered hub-failover list: [0] is the
112	                                   #   active concentrator, the rest ordered
113	                                   #   standbys. Each may be an ip:port literal
114	                                   #   (default) or a hostname with dns = true; when
115	                                   #   hostnames are used, each resolves to its full
116	                                   #   A+AAAA set and all addresses are ordered
117	                                   #   together in the failover list. duplicates
118	                                   #   rejected. Mutually exclusive with `endpoint`
119	                                   #   (its one-element form). Rejected on the
120	                                   #   concentrator.
121	allowed_ips = ["10.77.0.1/32"]     # REQUIRED: >= 1 CIDR routed to this peer
122	                                   #   (enforced when the WG UAPI is built). This is
123	                                   #   the peer's inner tunnel address(es). A literal
124	                                   #   0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 (full tunnel) is ALWAYS split
125	                                   #   into the equivalent /1+/1 pair at UAPI render —
126	                                   #   the engine never sees the literal /0.
127	# mode = "default-route"           # OPTIONAL, edge-only. Marks this peer as the
128	                                   #   edge's full-tunnel concentrator (opts into a
129	                                   #   0.0.0.0/0 / ::/0 allowed_ips entry above).
130	                                   #   Rejected on the concentrator. When set, the
131	                                   #   daemon installs this peer's allowed_ips split
132	                                   #   (wg-quick /1+/1 for 0.0.0.0/0) as scope-link
133	                                   #   routes via wanbond0 once the interface is up,
134	                                   #   and removes them on stop. Routes ONLY — no
135	                                   #   policy routing, SNAT, or concentrator
136	                                   #   forwarding (those stay operator-owned).
137	# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # OPTIONAL. Meaningful only for a MULTI-PEER
138	                                   #   concentrator (see the example below). With
139	                                   #   exactly one peer configured — as shown live
140	                                   #   here — a per-peer psk is REJECTED at load
141	                                   #   ("psk is not meaningful with a single peer");
142	                                   #   leave it UNSET and the top-level `psk` above
143	                                   #   is used, so a legacy single-peer config is
144	                                   #   unaffected. With MORE THAN ONE peer, this
145	                                   #   field becomes REQUIRED and must be pairwise-
146	                                   #   distinct across peers: each peer authenticates
147	                                   #   its own PROBE frames with its OWN psk, and the
148	                                   #   top-level `psk` authenticates NO peer once a
149	                                   #   second peer is configured (T80/T81, Q21).
150	                                   #   Generate: head -c 32 /dev/urandom | base64
151	# name = "edge1"                    # OPTIONAL. Unused when a single peer is
152	                                   #   configured, as shown live here (metrics never
153	                                   #   carry a `peer` label for a single bound peer,
154	                                   #   see docs/runbook.md's "Multi-peer concentrator
155	                                   #   (G4)" metrics note). With MORE THAN ONE peer,
156	                                   #   REQUIRED and must be unique across peers
157	                                   #   (T80/T81, Q21). ADDITIONAL peers (all but the
158	                                   #   first-configured) are exposed under this name
159	                                   #   as the metrics `peer` label; the first/primary
160	                                   #   peer's series always carry the empty label
161	                                   #   peer="" regardless of this field (D58).
162	
163	# ── second peer example (hostname endpoint with DNS opt-in) ──────────────────────
164	# [[wireguard.peers]]
165	# public_key = "<base64 peer public key>"
166	# endpoint = "concentrator.example.com:51820"  # hostname endpoint (requires dns = true)
167	# dns = true                        # enable hostname resolution for this endpoint
168	#                                   #   ([dns] block below is OPTIONAL — absent
169	#                                   #   means the system resolver; see [dns])
170	# allowed_ips = ["10.77.0.1/32"]
171	
172	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
173	# MULTI-PEER CONCENTRATOR EXAMPLE (G4): TWO EDGES, ONE CONCENTRATOR
174	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
175	#
176	# The plural [[wireguard.peers]] syntax (one block per edge) is supported on the
177	# concentrator. Single-peer configs (one [[wireguard.peers]] block, as shown
178	# live above) are unaffected: with exactly one peer, a per-peer `psk` is
179	# REJECTED at load (the top-level `psk` is used) and a per-peer `name` is
180	# optional and inert for metrics.
181	#
182	# With MORE THAN ONE peer, every peer authenticates its own PROBE frames with
183	# its OWN per-peer `psk` (internal/bind/multipath.go's peerBySource demux binds
184	# a source only once a PROBE MAC-verifies under that peer's psk); the
185	# top-level `psk` below remains REQUIRED by config validation but
186	# authenticates NO peer once a second peer is configured
187	# (internal/device/device.go feeds only per-peer PSKs to the bind — see
188	# docs/design.md's Security model). Each peer's `name` is REQUIRED and must be
189	# unique; ADDITIONAL peers (all but the first-configured) are exposed under
190	# their configured name as the metrics `peer` label, while the first/primary
191	# peer's series still carry the empty label peer="" — byte-compatible with the
192	# single-peer exposition, but a discrepancy versus the "name required" config
193	# rule filed as defect D58 (documented current behavior; not fixed here).
194	#
195	# ── live example (two edges, distinct per-peer psk/name) ─────────────────────
196	# role = "concentrator"
197	# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # REQUIRED by validate; authenticates no
198	                                   #   peer once a second peer is configured.
199	#
200	# [[paths]]
201	# name = "wan0"
202	# source_addr = "10.0.0.5"
203	#
204	# [wireguard]
205	# private_key = "<base64 32-byte private key>"
206	# listen_port = 51820
207	#
208	# # Edge 1: office — bound FIRST, so its metrics series carry peer="" (D58)
209	# # even though `name` is set and required in multi-peer mode.
210	# [[wireguard.peers]]
211	# public_key = "<base64 office-edge public key>"
212	# allowed_ips = ["10.77.1.0/24"]
213	# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK for office-edge>"    # REQUIRED in multi-peer mode
214	# name = "office-edge"                             # REQUIRED in multi-peer mode
215	#
216	# # Edge 2: mobile — registered via AddConcentratorPeer, so its metrics
217	# # series carry peer="mobile-edge".
218	# [[wireguard.peers]]
219	# public_key = "<base64 mobile-edge public key>"
220	# allowed_ips = ["10.77.2.0/24"]
221	# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK for mobile-edge>"    # Must differ from office-edge's psk
222	# name = "mobile-edge"                             # Must differ from office-edge's name
223	#
224	# [metrics]
225	# listen = "127.0.0.1:9090"
226	
227	# ── amnezia obfuscation: OPTIONAL, OFF by default (plain WireGuard) ───────────
228	# ALL-OR-NOTHING: either omit the whole block, or set the entire jc/jmin/jmax/s1/s2
229	# set — IDENTICALLY on both ends. One obfuscation engine per process.
230	# [amnezia]
231	# jc   = 4                         # junk packet count. > 0 when configured.
232	# jmin = 40                        # min junk packet size. > 0; jmin <= jmax.
233	# jmax = 70                        # max junk packet size. > 0.
234	# s1   = 30                        # init-packet junk prefix size. > 0.
235	# s2   = 40                        # response-packet junk prefix size. > 0.
236	                                   #   Constraint: (148 + s1) != (92 + s2) — the
237	                                   #   obfuscated init/response lengths must differ.
238	                                   #   (no defaults: required when the block present.)
239	# h1 = 1                           # magic header: initiation. Default 1..4 when the
240	# h2 = 2                           #   block is configured but NO header is given.
241	# h3 = 3                           #   When any is set, h1..h4 must be a DISTINCT set
242	# h4 = 4                           #   (values <= 4 mean "standard message type").
243	
244	# ── scheduler: OPTIONAL. Omitted => active-backup, all knobs below ignored ────
245	# [scheduler]
246	# policy = "active-backup"         # "active-backup" (DEFAULT) | "weighted". Every
247	                                   #   knob below applies ONLY to "weighted"; under
248	                                   #   active-backup they are inert and left unset.
249	# per_path_capacity_fps = 10000.0  # DEFAULT 10000. Reference per-path capacity
250	                                   #   (frame slots/s): aggregation-gate denominator
251	                                   #   and pacing refill rate. > 0.
252	# engage_fraction    = 0.9         # DEFAULT 0.9. Engage aggregation above
253	                                   #   engage_fraction * capacity. In (0, 1].
254	# disengage_fraction = 0.5         # DEFAULT 0.5. Collapse below disengage_fraction
255	                                   #   * capacity. Must be in [0, engage_fraction) —
256	                                   #   the hysteresis band.
257	# collapse_dwell = "2s"            # DEFAULT 2s. Sustained-low dwell before
258	                                   #   collapsing to primary-only. >= 0.
259	# load_tau = "200ms"               # DEFAULT 200ms. Offered-load rate estimator time
260	                                   #   constant. > 0.
261	# pacing_enabled = false           # DEFAULT false. Turn on per-path send-pacing
262	                                   #   (token buckets). Off => buckets bypassed.
263	# pacing_burst_frames = 64.0       # DEFAULT 64. Token-bucket burst (frames). > 0
264	                                   #   when pacing_enabled.
265	# weight_rtt_floor = "1ms"         # DEFAULT 1ms. RTT floor in the weight formula. >0.
266	# weight_loss_floor = 0.001        # DEFAULT 1e-3. Loss floor under the sqrt in the
267	                                   #   weight formula. > 0.
268	
269	# ── fec: OPTIONAL, OFF by default (no parity on the wire) ─────────────────────
270	# [fec]
271	# enabled = true                   # DEFAULT false. Turns the FEC plane on; when
272	                                   #   false every field below is ignored.
273	# data_shards = 10                 # K: inner datagrams per coding group. >= 1 when
274	                                   #   enabled. No default (required).
275	# parity_shards = 6                # M: parity frames per group (and the adaptive
276	                                   #   parity CEILING). >= 1 when enabled; K + M <=
277	                                   #   256. No default (required).
278	# deadline = "5ms"                 # DEFAULT 5ms. Partial-group flush deadline. > 0
279	                                   #   and <= 125ms.
280	# adaptive = false                 # DEFAULT false. Closed-loop parity controller
281	                                   #   (parity tracks measured loss up to the
282	                                   #   ceiling). Requires enabled = true.
283	# target_residual = 0.005          # ADAPTIVE-ONLY, primary sizing surface:
284	                                   #   post-recovery residual-loss SLA in (0, 1). No
285	                                   #   default. Mutually exclusive with safety_factor.
286	# safety_factor = 1.5              # ADAPTIVE-ONLY, legacy headroom multiplier >= 1.
287	                                   #   DEFAULTS to 1.5 when adaptive and NEITHER field
288	                                   #   is set. Must stay 0 in fixed mode. Mutually
289	                                   #   exclusive with target_residual.
290	
291	# ── dns: OPTIONAL. Omitted => system resolver, default cadence/timeouts ───────
292	# Selects the TRANSPORT a peer's opt-in hostname endpoint (dns = true, above)
293	# is resolved through. [dns] alone never turns hostname resolution on.
294	#
295	# EXAMPLE 1 (IP-literal DoH resolver):
296	# [dns]
297	# resolver = "doh"                 # "system" | "doh" | "dot"
298	# doh_url = "https://198.51.100.1/dns-query"  # REQUIRED iff resolver = "doh"
299	# poll_interval = "30s"            # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT 30s. Re-resolution cadence.
300	# timeout = "5s"                   # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT 5s. Per-lookup timeout.
301	#
302	# EXAMPLE 2 (hostname DoT resolver, requires bootstrap_ip):
303	# [dns]
304	# resolver = "dot"                 # "system" | "doh" | "dot"
305	# dot_server = "dns.example.com:853"  # REQUIRED iff resolver = "dot"
306	# bootstrap_ip = "198.51.100.2"    # REQUIRED: dot_server's host is a hostname
307	                                   #   (BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant: must reach the
308	                                   #   resolver WITHOUT DNS lookup itself).
309	# poll_interval = "30s"
310	# timeout = "5s"
311	#
312	# EXAMPLE 3 (system resolver, any IP-literal endpoint):
313	# [dns]
314	# resolver = "system"              # DEFAULT "system". No URL/server/bootstrap needed.
315	# poll_interval = "30s"
316	# timeout = "5s"
317	#
318	# Each EXAMPLE above is self-contained and independently valid: uncomment
319	# EXACTLY ONE (its [dns] header plus its fields) and nothing else. The three
320	# resolver modes are mutually exclusive — setting fields from more than one
321	# EXAMPLE at once (e.g. resolver = "doh" together with dot_server, or a
322	# bootstrap_ip on a doh_url/dot_server host that is already an IP literal) is
323	# rejected at config load. See internal/config/dns.go for every field's exact
324	# precondition.
325	
326	# ── metrics: OPTIONAL. Omit the block => no /metrics endpoint is served ───────
327	[metrics]
328	listen = "127.0.0.1:9090"          # No default. LOOPBACK-ONLY (127.0.0.0/8, ::1, or
329	                                   #   a hostname resolving only to loopback); any
330	                                   #   other address is REFUSED when the endpoint
331	                                   #   binds (not at load).
332	
333	# ── log: OPTIONAL ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
334	[log]
335	level = "info"                     # DEFAULT "info" (empty => info). One of
336	                                   #   debug | info | warn | error ("warning"
337	                                   #   accepted for warn). Unknown => fail-fast.
338	
339	# ── Cross-field constraints (not expressible inline) ─────────────────────────
340	# • Roles: `role` is required, never inferred. Edge-only: per-peer endpoint/
341	#   endpoints (required on edge, rejected on concentrator) + the pacing
342	#   declarations (dest_addr is edge-only in effect, merely inert on the
343	#   concentrator). Concentrator-only: wireguard.listen_port (required there).
344	# • endpoint vs endpoints: mutually exclusive; `endpoint` is the one-element form
345	#   of `endpoints`. On the edge exactly one must be present; the concentrator sets
346	#   neither. Each entry is an ip:port literal (default) or, with the peer's
347	#   `dns = true` opt-in, a hostname:port resolved at runtime (never at config
348	#   load). Duplicates are rejected within each form (literal-vs-literal,
349	#   hostname:port-vs-hostname:port) — a hostname is never compared against a
350	#   literal at load time.
351	# • Distinct source_addr: each [[paths]] needs a unique, valid source_addr
352	#   (compared unmapped, so 192.0.2.10 and ::ffff:192.0.2.10 collide). `name` must
353	#   also be unique.
354	# • allowed_ips: >= 1 CIDR per peer; empty is rejected when the WG config is
355	#   assembled (not by config.validate).
356	# • amnezia all-or-nothing: an unconfigured (all-zero) block is plain WireGuard.
357	#   Once ANY of jc/jmin/jmax/s1/s2/h1..h4 is set, the full jc,jmin,jmax,s1,s2 set
358	#   must be > 0, jmin <= jmax, 148+s1 != 92+s2, and h1..h4 must be a distinct set
359	#   (omitted headers default to 1..4). Same profile on both ends. One engine/process.
360	# • link_bandwidth + link_rtt are a pair: both-or-neither, and all-or-nothing
361	#   across every path (declare on every [[paths]] block or none). They take effect
362	#   ONLY under scheduler.policy = "weighted" with pacing_enabled = true; otherwise
363	#   a declared bandwidth is inert. When active they are mutually exclusive with the
364	#   raw per_path_capacity_fps / pacing_burst_frames knobs.
365	# • scheduler off-unless-present: no [scheduler] => active-backup, every weighted
366	#   knob ignored. Under weighted, the hysteresis band requires
367	#   disengage_fraction < engage_fraction.
368	# • fec off-unless-present: no [fec] (or enabled = false) => no parity. adaptive =
369	#   true requires enabled = true. In adaptive mode set EITHER target_residual OR
370	#   safety_factor, never both; both must stay 0 (unset) in fixed mode.
371	# • metrics loopback-only: listen must be a loopback address (or a hostname
372	#   resolving entirely to loopback); a non-loopback bind is refused when the
373	#   endpoint starts. Omit the block to serve no metrics.
374	# • bind (top-level default + per-path override): "source" | "device" | "auto"
375	#   (DEFAULT). A [[paths]] block with no `bind` uses the top-level default
376	#   (itself DEFAULT "auto" when also omitted, so an existing config with no
377	#   `bind` anywhere is unaffected). "auto" reproduces today's selectDeviceBinds
378	#   heuristic; "source" and "device" force one behavior unconditionally. See
379	#   install.md §3b for the VLAN-per-WAN policy-routing case `bind = "source"`
380	#   exists to fix.
381	# • dns off-unless-present: no [dns] => system resolver, default cadence/
382	#   timeouts; [dns] only selects the transport a peer's `dns = true` opt-in
383	#   uses, never a gate by itself. doh_url/dot_server are mode-specific: set
384	#   exactly the one matching `resolver`, the other (and bootstrap_ip under
385	#   resolver = "system") must stay absent. BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant: a
386	#   hostname-form doh_url/dot_server host REQUIRES bootstrap_ip; an IP
387	#   literal host REJECTS a non-empty bootstrap_ip (mode mismatch).
388

file.content:
# wanbond.example.toml — exhaustive annotated configuration reference.
#
# Every key wanbond reads is present here. Copy this file, fill the <placeholders>,
# and delete what you do not need:
#
#   sudo install -m 0600 /dev/null /etc/wanbond/edge.toml
#   $EDITOR /etc/wanbond/edge.toml     # paste + edit this content
#
# 0600 is MANDATORY: the file holds the WireGuard private key and the PSK, so
# config.Load refuses any file whose mode is not exactly 0600.
#
# This example is EDGE-oriented. For a CONCENTRATOR: set role = "concentrator",
# uncomment [wireguard].listen_port, and REMOVE the peer `endpoint`/`endpoints`
# (the concentrator learns the edge's endpoints from authenticated traffic).
#
# Required keys are shown live; optional keys are commented-out with their
# DEFAULT value (uncommenting one at its shown value is a no-op). Cross-field
# constraints a single example cannot express inline are listed at the bottom.
# Full prose reference: docs/install.md §3z. Everything here is enforced at
# config load (fail-fast on the first violation) except the loopback + allowed_ips
# checks noted below.

# ── top level ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
role = "edge"                      # REQUIRED. "edge" | "concentrator". Never
                                   #   inferred from other keys.
psk  = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # REQUIRED. 32 raw bytes, base64. SAME value on
                                   #   both ends; keys the PSK-HMAC that
                                   #   authenticates outer PROBE/CONTROL frames.
                                   #   Generate: head -c 32 /dev/urandom | base64
# tun_persist = false              # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT false. When false the TUN
                                   #   wanbond0 is destroyed on daemon stop, so its
                                   #   addresses/routes/rules are dropped on every
                                   #   restart (today's teardown semantics). Set
                                   #   true and the daemon issues TUNSETPERSIST on
                                   #   start: wanbond0 SURVIVES a daemon restart
                                   #   with the SAME ifindex, so operator-owned
                                   #   addressing persists untouched. NOTE (D39): a
                                   #   persistent device STILL needs the
                                   #   NetworkManager unmanaged-devices drop-in on
                                   #   NM hosts — persistence keeps the link, it
                                   #   does not exempt it from NM.
# bind = "auto"                    # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT "auto". Top-level default
                                   #   bind mode, applied to every [[paths]] block
                                   #   that omits its own `bind` below. "source" |
                                   #   "device" | "auto" — see the per-path `bind`
                                   #   note below for what each value does. Exists
                                   #   for the VLAN-per-WAN policy-routing case;
                                   #   see docs/install.md §3b.

# ── paths: one [[paths]] block per WAN uplink; at least one is REQUIRED ───────
[[paths]]
name        = "starlink"           # REQUIRED. Stable, unique identifier.
source_addr = "192.168.1.10"       # REQUIRED. Bare local source IP the path's UDP
                                   #   socket binds to. Must be unique across paths
                                   #   (a shared source collides EADDRINUSE at the
                                   #   second bind). No default.
# bind = "auto"                    # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT the top-level default above
                                   #   (itself DEFAULT "auto" when that is also
                                   #   omitted). "source" forces the pre-T16
                                   #   source-IP pin unconditionally (defeats
                                   #   device-bind roam tolerance, but is the fix
                                   #   for a one-address-per-VLAN policy-routing
                                   #   edge — see docs/install.md §3b). "device"
                                   #   forces SO_BINDTODEVICE unconditionally.
                                   #   "auto" reproduces today's heuristic:
                                   #   device-bind only when provably equivalent
                                   #   to the source-IP pin, source-bind otherwise.
# dest_addr = "203.0.113.7:51820"  # OPTIONAL, edge-only meaning. Per-path
                                   #   concentrator endpoint (ip:port). Omit when
                                   #   one public IP fronts all uplinks (the peer's
                                   #   endpoint is reused). No default. Inert on the
                                   #   concentrator (it learns edge endpoints).
# link_bandwidth = "50Mbit"        # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared bottleneck
                                   #   bandwidth. SI bit/s: k/M/G = 1e3/1e6/1e9
                                   #   ("bit" may be written "bps"). Must be > 0.
                                   #   Used ONLY under weighted policy + pacing;
                                   #   inert otherwise. No default (undeclared).
# link_rtt = "45ms"                # OPTIONAL. Operator-declared baseline RTT (Go
                                   #   duration). REQUIRED (> 0) when link_bandwidth
                                   #   is set under weighted pacing; else ignored.

# A second uplink (edge). Repeat the block per path.
[[paths]]
name        = "5g"
source_addr = "192.168.2.10"

# ── wireguard: inner tunnel key material ─────────────────────────────────────
[wireguard]
private_key = "<base64 32-byte private key>"  # REQUIRED (both roles).
                                   #   Generate: wg genkey (public: … | wg pubkey)
# listen_port = 51820              # CONCENTRATOR-ONLY: REQUIRED (> 0) there; omit /
                                   #   leave 0 on the edge. uint16 (< 1024 needs
                                   #   CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE).

# ── wireguard peers: at least one [[wireguard.peers]] is REQUIRED ─────────────
[[wireguard.peers]]
public_key = "<base64 peer public key>"  # REQUIRED. base64 32-byte key (the OTHER
                                   #   end's public key).
endpoint = "203.0.113.7:51820"     # EDGE: REQUIRED (this OR `endpoints`). ip:port
                                   #   literal (default) OR hostname with opt-in
                                   #   `dns = true` below. CONCENTRATOR: must be
                                   #   ABSENT (rejected) — it roams the edge
                                   #   dynamically. Mutually exclusive with
                                   #   `endpoints`.
# dns = false                       # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT false. Set true to enable
                                   #   hostname resolution for the endpoint above.
                                   #   The [dns] block below is OPTIONAL: it only
                                   #   selects the resolver TRANSPORT and defaults
                                   #   to the system resolver when absent.
# endpoints = ["203.0.113.7:51820", "198.51.100.7:51820"]
                                   # EDGE-ONLY ordered hub-failover list: [0] is the
                                   #   active concentrator, the rest ordered
                                   #   standbys. Each may be an ip:port literal
                                   #   (default) or a hostname with dns = true; when
                                   #   hostnames are used, each resolves to its full
                                   #   A+AAAA set and all addresses are ordered
                                   #   together in the failover list. duplicates
                                   #   rejected. Mutually exclusive with `endpoint`
                                   #   (its one-element form). Rejected on the
                                   #   concentrator.
allowed_ips = ["10.77.0.1/32"]     # REQUIRED: >= 1 CIDR routed to this peer
                                   #   (enforced when the WG UAPI is built). This is
                                   #   the peer's inner tunnel address(es). A literal
                                   #   0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0 (full tunnel) is ALWAYS split
                                   #   into the equivalent /1+/1 pair at UAPI render —
                                   #   the engine never sees the literal /0.
# mode = "default-route"           # OPTIONAL, edge-only. Marks this peer as the
                                   #   edge's full-tunnel concentrator (opts into a
                                   #   0.0.0.0/0 / ::/0 allowed_ips entry above).
                                   #   Rejected on the concentrator. When set, the
                                   #   daemon installs this peer's allowed_ips split
                                   #   (wg-quick /1+/1 for 0.0.0.0/0) as scope-link
                                   #   routes via wanbond0 once the interface is up,
                                   #   and removes them on stop. Routes ONLY — no
                                   #   policy routing, SNAT, or concentrator
                                   #   forwarding (those stay operator-owned).
# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # OPTIONAL. Meaningful only for a MULTI-PEER
                                   #   concentrator (see the example below). With
                                   #   exactly one peer configured — as shown live
                                   #   here — a per-peer psk is REJECTED at load
                                   #   ("psk is not meaningful with a single peer");
                                   #   leave it UNSET and the top-level `psk` above
                                   #   is used, so a legacy single-peer config is
                                   #   unaffected. With MORE THAN ONE peer, this
                                   #   field becomes REQUIRED and must be pairwise-
                                   #   distinct across peers: each peer authenticates
                                   #   its own PROBE frames with its OWN psk, and the
                                   #   top-level `psk` authenticates NO peer once a
                                   #   second peer is configured (T80/T81, Q21).
                                   #   Generate: head -c 32 /dev/urandom | base64
# name = "edge1"                    # OPTIONAL. Unused when a single peer is
                                   #   configured, as shown live here (metrics never
                                   #   carry a `peer` label for a single bound peer,
                                   #   see docs/runbook.md's "Multi-peer concentrator
                                   #   (G4)" metrics note). With MORE THAN ONE peer,
                                   #   REQUIRED and must be unique across peers
                                   #   (T80/T81, Q21). ADDITIONAL peers (all but the
                                   #   first-configured) are exposed under this name
                                   #   as the metrics `peer` label; the first/primary
                                   #   peer's series always carry the empty label
                                   #   peer="" regardless of this field (D58).

# ── second peer example (hostname endpoint with DNS opt-in) ──────────────────────
# [[wireguard.peers]]
# public_key = "<base64 peer public key>"
# endpoint = "concentrator.example.com:51820"  # hostname endpoint (requires dns = true)
# dns = true                        # enable hostname resolution for this endpoint
#                                   #   ([dns] block below is OPTIONAL — absent
#                                   #   means the system resolver; see [dns])
# allowed_ips = ["10.77.0.1/32"]

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# MULTI-PEER CONCENTRATOR EXAMPLE (G4): TWO EDGES, ONE CONCENTRATOR
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
#
# The plural [[wireguard.peers]] syntax (one block per edge) is supported on the
# concentrator. Single-peer configs (one [[wireguard.peers]] block, as shown
# live above) are unaffected: with exactly one peer, a per-peer `psk` is
# REJECTED at load (the top-level `psk` is used) and a per-peer `name` is
# optional and inert for metrics.
#
# With MORE THAN ONE peer, every peer authenticates its own PROBE frames with
# its OWN per-peer `psk` (internal/bind/multipath.go's peerBySource demux binds
# a source only once a PROBE MAC-verifies under that peer's psk); the
# top-level `psk` below remains REQUIRED by config validation but
# authenticates NO peer once a second peer is configured
# (internal/device/device.go feeds only per-peer PSKs to the bind — see
# docs/design.md's Security model). Each peer's `name` is REQUIRED and must be
# unique; ADDITIONAL peers (all but the first-configured) are exposed under
# their configured name as the metrics `peer` label, while the first/primary
# peer's series still carry the empty label peer="" — byte-compatible with the
# single-peer exposition, but a discrepancy versus the "name required" config
# rule filed as defect D58 (documented current behavior; not fixed here).
#
# ── live example (two edges, distinct per-peer psk/name) ─────────────────────
# role = "concentrator"
# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK>"      # REQUIRED by validate; authenticates no
                                   #   peer once a second peer is configured.
#
# [[paths]]
# name = "wan0"
# source_addr = "10.0.0.5"
#
# [wireguard]
# private_key = "<base64 32-byte private key>"
# listen_port = 51820
#
# # Edge 1: office — bound FIRST, so its metrics series carry peer="" (D58)
# # even though `name` is set and required in multi-peer mode.
# [[wireguard.peers]]
# public_key = "<base64 office-edge public key>"
# allowed_ips = ["10.77.1.0/24"]
# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK for office-edge>"    # REQUIRED in multi-peer mode
# name = "office-edge"                             # REQUIRED in multi-peer mode
#
# # Edge 2: mobile — registered via AddConcentratorPeer, so its metrics
# # series carry peer="mobile-edge".
# [[wireguard.peers]]
# public_key = "<base64 mobile-edge public key>"
# allowed_ips = ["10.77.2.0/24"]
# psk = "<base64 32-byte PSK for mobile-edge>"    # Must differ from office-edge's psk
# name = "mobile-edge"                             # Must differ from office-edge's name
#
# [metrics]
# listen = "127.0.0.1:9090"

# ── amnezia obfuscation: OPTIONAL, OFF by default (plain WireGuard) ───────────
# ALL-OR-NOTHING: either omit the whole block, or set the entire jc/jmin/jmax/s1/s2
# set — IDENTICALLY on both ends. One obfuscation engine per process.
# [amnezia]
# jc   = 4                         # junk packet count. > 0 when configured.
# jmin = 40                        # min junk packet size. > 0; jmin <= jmax.
# jmax = 70                        # max junk packet size. > 0.
# s1   = 30                        # init-packet junk prefix size. > 0.
# s2   = 40                        # response-packet junk prefix size. > 0.
                                   #   Constraint: (148 + s1) != (92 + s2) — the
                                   #   obfuscated init/response lengths must differ.
                                   #   (no defaults: required when the block present.)
# h1 = 1                           # magic header: initiation. Default 1..4 when the
# h2 = 2                           #   block is configured but NO header is given.
# h3 = 3                           #   When any is set, h1..h4 must be a DISTINCT set
# h4 = 4                           #   (values <= 4 mean "standard message type").

# ── scheduler: OPTIONAL. Omitted => active-backup, all knobs below ignored ────
# [scheduler]
# policy = "active-backup"         # "active-backup" (DEFAULT) | "weighted". Every
                                   #   knob below applies ONLY to "weighted"; under
                                   #   active-backup they are inert and left unset.
# per_path_capacity_fps = 10000.0  # DEFAULT 10000. Reference per-path capacity
                                   #   (frame slots/s): aggregation-gate denominator
                                   #   and pacing refill rate. > 0.
# engage_fraction    = 0.9         # DEFAULT 0.9. Engage aggregation above
                                   #   engage_fraction * capacity. In (0, 1].
# disengage_fraction = 0.5         # DEFAULT 0.5. Collapse below disengage_fraction
                                   #   * capacity. Must be in [0, engage_fraction) —
                                   #   the hysteresis band.
# collapse_dwell = "2s"            # DEFAULT 2s. Sustained-low dwell before
                                   #   collapsing to primary-only. >= 0.
# load_tau = "200ms"               # DEFAULT 200ms. Offered-load rate estimator time
                                   #   constant. > 0.
# pacing_enabled = false           # DEFAULT false. Turn on per-path send-pacing
                                   #   (token buckets). Off => buckets bypassed.
# pacing_burst_frames = 64.0       # DEFAULT 64. Token-bucket burst (frames). > 0
                                   #   when pacing_enabled.
# weight_rtt_floor = "1ms"         # DEFAULT 1ms. RTT floor in the weight formula. >0.
# weight_loss_floor = 0.001        # DEFAULT 1e-3. Loss floor under the sqrt in the
                                   #   weight formula. > 0.

# ── fec: OPTIONAL, OFF by default (no parity on the wire) ─────────────────────
# [fec]
# enabled = true                   # DEFAULT false. Turns the FEC plane on; when
                                   #   false every field below is ignored.
# data_shards = 10                 # K: inner datagrams per coding group. >= 1 when
                                   #   enabled. No default (required).
# parity_shards = 6                # M: parity frames per group (and the adaptive
                                   #   parity CEILING). >= 1 when enabled; K + M <=
                                   #   256. No default (required).
# deadline = "5ms"                 # DEFAULT 5ms. Partial-group flush deadline. > 0
                                   #   and <= 125ms.
# adaptive = false                 # DEFAULT false. Closed-loop parity controller
                                   #   (parity tracks measured loss up to the
                                   #   ceiling). Requires enabled = true.
# target_residual = 0.005          # ADAPTIVE-ONLY, primary sizing surface:
                                   #   post-recovery residual-loss SLA in (0, 1). No
                                   #   default. Mutually exclusive with safety_factor.
# safety_factor = 1.5              # ADAPTIVE-ONLY, legacy headroom multiplier >= 1.
                                   #   DEFAULTS to 1.5 when adaptive and NEITHER field
                                   #   is set. Must stay 0 in fixed mode. Mutually
                                   #   exclusive with target_residual.

# ── dns: OPTIONAL. Omitted => system resolver, default cadence/timeouts ───────
# Selects the TRANSPORT a peer's opt-in hostname endpoint (dns = true, above)
# is resolved through. [dns] alone never turns hostname resolution on.
#
# EXAMPLE 1 (IP-literal DoH resolver):
# [dns]
# resolver = "doh"                 # "system" | "doh" | "dot"
# doh_url = "https://198.51.100.1/dns-query"  # REQUIRED iff resolver = "doh"
# poll_interval = "30s"            # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT 30s. Re-resolution cadence.
# timeout = "5s"                   # OPTIONAL, DEFAULT 5s. Per-lookup timeout.
#
# EXAMPLE 2 (hostname DoT resolver, requires bootstrap_ip):
# [dns]
# resolver = "dot"                 # "system" | "doh" | "dot"
# dot_server = "dns.example.com:853"  # REQUIRED iff resolver = "dot"
# bootstrap_ip = "198.51.100.2"    # REQUIRED: dot_server's host is a hostname
                                   #   (BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant: must reach the
                                   #   resolver WITHOUT DNS lookup itself).
# poll_interval = "30s"
# timeout = "5s"
#
# EXAMPLE 3 (system resolver, any IP-literal endpoint):
# [dns]
# resolver = "system"              # DEFAULT "system". No URL/server/bootstrap needed.
# poll_interval = "30s"
# timeout = "5s"
#
# Each EXAMPLE above is self-contained and independently valid: uncomment
# EXACTLY ONE (its [dns] header plus its fields) and nothing else. The three
# resolver modes are mutually exclusive — setting fields from more than one
# EXAMPLE at once (e.g. resolver = "doh" together with dot_server, or a
# bootstrap_ip on a doh_url/dot_server host that is already an IP literal) is
# rejected at config load. See internal/config/dns.go for every field's exact
# precondition.

# ── metrics: OPTIONAL. Omit the block => no /metrics endpoint is served ───────
[metrics]
listen = "127.0.0.1:9090"          # No default. LOOPBACK-ONLY (127.0.0.0/8, ::1, or
                                   #   a hostname resolving only to loopback); any
                                   #   other address is REFUSED when the endpoint
                                   #   binds (not at load).

# ── log: OPTIONAL ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[log]
level = "info"                     # DEFAULT "info" (empty => info). One of
                                   #   debug | info | warn | error ("warning"
                                   #   accepted for warn). Unknown => fail-fast.

# ── Cross-field constraints (not expressible inline) ─────────────────────────
# • Roles: `role` is required, never inferred. Edge-only: per-peer endpoint/
#   endpoints (required on edge, rejected on concentrator) + the pacing
#   declarations (dest_addr is edge-only in effect, merely inert on the
#   concentrator). Concentrator-only: wireguard.listen_port (required there).
# • endpoint vs endpoints: mutually exclusive; `endpoint` is the one-element form
#   of `endpoints`. On the edge exactly one must be present; the concentrator sets
#   neither. Each entry is an ip:port literal (default) or, with the peer's
#   `dns = true` opt-in, a hostname:port resolved at runtime (never at config
#   load). Duplicates are rejected within each form (literal-vs-literal,
#   hostname:port-vs-hostname:port) — a hostname is never compared against a
#   literal at load time.
# • Distinct source_addr: each [[paths]] needs a unique, valid source_addr
#   (compared unmapped, so 192.0.2.10 and ::ffff:192.0.2.10 collide). `name` must
#   also be unique.
# • allowed_ips: >= 1 CIDR per peer; empty is rejected when the WG config is
#   assembled (not by config.validate).
# • amnezia all-or-nothing: an unconfigured (all-zero) block is plain WireGuard.
#   Once ANY of jc/jmin/jmax/s1/s2/h1..h4 is set, the full jc,jmin,jmax,s1,s2 set
#   must be > 0, jmin <= jmax, 148+s1 != 92+s2, and h1..h4 must be a distinct set
#   (omitted headers default to 1..4). Same profile on both ends. One engine/process.
# • link_bandwidth + link_rtt are a pair: both-or-neither, and all-or-nothing
#   across every path (declare on every [[paths]] block or none). They take effect
#   ONLY under scheduler.policy = "weighted" with pacing_enabled = true; otherwise
#   a declared bandwidth is inert. When active they are mutually exclusive with the
#   raw per_path_capacity_fps / pacing_burst_frames knobs.
# • scheduler off-unless-present: no [scheduler] => active-backup, every weighted
#   knob ignored. Under weighted, the hysteresis band requires
#   disengage_fraction < engage_fraction.
# • fec off-unless-present: no [fec] (or enabled = false) => no parity. adaptive =
#   true requires enabled = true. In adaptive mode set EITHER target_residual OR
#   safety_factor, never both; both must stay 0 (unset) in fixed mode.
# • metrics loopback-only: listen must be a loopback address (or a hostname
#   resolving entirely to loopback); a non-loopback bind is refused when the
#   endpoint starts. Omit the block to serve no metrics.
# • bind (top-level default + per-path override): "source" | "device" | "auto"
#   (DEFAULT). A [[paths]] block with no `bind` uses the top-level default
#   (itself DEFAULT "auto" when also omitted, so an existing config with no
#   `bind` anywhere is unaffected). "auto" reproduces today's selectDeviceBinds
#   heuristic; "source" and "device" force one behavior unconditionally. See
#   install.md §3b for the VLAN-per-WAN policy-routing case `bind = "source"`
#   exists to fix.
# • dns off-unless-present: no [dns] => system resolver, default cadence/
#   timeouts; [dns] only selects the transport a peer's `dns = true` opt-in
#   uses, never a gate by itself. doh_url/dot_server are mode-specific: set
#   exactly the one matching `resolver`, the other (and bootstrap_ip under
#   resolver = "system") must stay absent. BOOTSTRAP-IP invariant: a
#   hostname-form doh_url/dot_server host REQUIRES bootstrap_ip; an IP
#   literal host REJECTS a non-empty bootstrap_ip (mode mismatch).
